data.title: US FDA proposes excluding weight-loss drugs from compounding list
data.description:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration on Thursday proposed excluding active ingredients for Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly's weight-loss drugs on the list of drugs outsourcing facilities can use for compounding.

If finalized, the exclusion would limit compounding of these medicines unless they appear on the agency's drug shortage list.

The FDA said it did not identify a clinical need for outsourcing facilities to compound semaglutide, the active ingredient of Novo's Wegovy and Ozempic, and tirzepatide, sold as Zepbound and Mounjaro by Lilly, from bulk drug substances. The proposal also includes Novo's older molecule liraglutide.

"When FDA-approved drugs are available, outsourcing facilities cannot lawfully compound using bulk drug substances unless there is a clear clinical need," FDA Commissioner Marty Makary said.

data.title: ECB Presents Balanced Risks — Market Talk
data.description: The European Central Bank kept its key rate unchanged as expected, flagging symmetrical risks of higher inflation and lower growth, says Deutsche Bank's Mark Wall. The bank instilled a sense of calm confidence in its accompanying statement, with references to the resilience of the economy in recent quarters and longer-term inflation expectations remaining well-anchored, the chief European economist says. "But there is also a sense of rising concern the longer the conflict in the Middle East continues." The ECB's statement doesn't pre-commit to a hike in June, but it doesn't present a case for preventing that either, he says. (edward.frankl@wsj.com)

data.title: Canada Produces Decent GDP Report, Although Struggles Remain — Market Talk
data.description: Canada's economy continues its streak of decent economic data, with GDP climbing 0.2% in February from the prior month, says Benjamin Reitzes, economist at BMO Capital Markets. Reitzes, however, offers a caveat, noting only eight of 20 sectors tracked posted growth in February, "highlighting that the economy continues to struggle despite the decent headline." Meanwhile, Canada's nonfarm payrolls report suggests that employers shed over 60,000 jobs in February--which underscores BOC's description of labor market as soft, Reitzes adds. All told, "the economy continues to hang in there despite the ongoing dark cloud of trade uncertainty," Reitzes says, adding the data does not move the needle for the Bank of Canada when it comes to rates. (Paul.Vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

data.title: Audited Annual Results for the Year Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
TORONTO, ON / ACCESS Newswire / April 30, 2026 / Sintana Energy Inc. (TSX-V:SEI)(AIM:SEI)(OTCQX:SEUSF) ("Sintana" or the "Company") is pleased to announce its audited Annual Results for the year ended December 31, 2025.

The 2025 Annual Report is available on the Company's website: https://sintanaenergy.com/

For further information, please contact:

Sintana Energy Inc

Robert Bose, Chief Executive Officer

Eytan Uliel, President

Tel: +44 (0)7 747 845 987

Zeus - Nomad and Joint Broker

Antonio Bossi / Darshan Patel / George Duxberry

Simon Johnson (Broking)

Tel: +44 (0) 20 3829 5000

Cavendish Capital Markets Limited - Joint Broker

Neil McDonald / Derrick Lee / Pearl Kellie

Tel: +44 (0) 20 3493 8000

Jonathan Paterson - Investor Relations

jonathan.paterson@harbor-access.com

Tel: +1 475 477 9401

CAMARCO - Financial PR

Billy Clegg / Georgia Edmonds / Sam Morris

Tel: +44 (0) 20 3757 4980

About Sintana Energy

Sintana Energy is an Atlantic Margin-focused oil and gas company, holding interests in a diverse portfolio of high-impact assets that spans the Southern Atlantic conjugate margin. The Company's current portfolio is strategically positioned in the emerging frontier geographies of Namibia, Uruguay and Angola, with additional legacy assets in Colombia and The Bahamas. Led by an experienced team, Sintana Energy is partnered with major industry players, and benefits from significant carry support, on key licenses across multiple jurisdictions. Sintana Energy is listed on the TSX-V in Canada under the symbol "SEI", in the United Kingdom on the LSE-AIM under the symbol "SEI" and in the U.S. on the OTCQX under the symbol "SEUSF".

For further information, please visit sintanaenergy.com

Forward-Looking Statements

The information provided in this announcement contains certain forward-looking statements and information (collectively, "forward-looking statements") within the meaning of applicable securities laws. Such forward-looking statements include, without limitation, forecasts, estimates, expectations and objectives for future operations that are subject to assumptions, risks and uncertainties, many of which are beyond the control of Sintana. Forward-looking statements are predictive in nature, depend upon or refer to future events or conditions, or include words such as "expect", "plan", "anticipate", "believe", "intend", "maintain", "continue to", "pursue", "design", "result in", "sustain" "estimate", "potential", "growth", "near-term", "long-term", "forecast", "contingent" and similar expressions, or are events or conditions that "will", "would", "may", "could" or "should" occur or be achieved. The forward-looking statements contained in this announcement speak only as of the date hereof and are expressly qualified by this cautionary statement.

Forward-looking statements are based upon, among other things, factors, expectations and assumptions that Sintana has made as at the date of this announcement regarding, among other things, the receipt of all applicable regulatory approvals and the anticipated schedule for receipt of funds pursuant to the Settlement Agreement.

Undue reliance should not be placed on the forward-looking statements because no assurance can be given that they will prove to be correct. Since forward-looking statements address future events and conditions, by their very nature they involve inherent risks and uncertainties. These risks include, but are not limited to, the ability of Sintana to receive all necessary regulatory approvals and third party satisfaction of all conditions of the Settlement Agreement.

Except as may be required by applicable securities laws, Sintana does not assume any obligation or intent to update publicly or revise any forward-looking statements made herein, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise.

data.title: ZAWYA: Etihad Guest introduces discount pricing for its premium members
data.description:
Abu Dhabi, UAE – Etihad Guest, the award-winning loyalty programme of Etihad Airways, enhances its exclusive member experience with additional benefits to the programme’s Promo seats, for Gold, Platinum and Emerald status Etihad Guest members.

Etihad’s Promo seats are exclusively available to every Etihad Guest member, providing exceptional value by offering access to fixed redemption mileage fares on flights across Etihad’s global network.

Etihad Guest now offers exclusive discounts of up to 10% off the regular Promo seat fare for Etihad Guest members with Gold, Platinum and Emerald membership status, offering even greater value to the programme’s most loyal members.

Mark Potter, Managing Director Etihad Guest at Etihad Airways, said, “Etihad Guest strives to offer exceptional experiences and bespoke benefits exclusively for our members. The enhancements to our Promo seats are designed to offer even greater value to our most engaged members. Introducing exclusive discounts allows us to ensure our members enjoy greater value and more rewarding redemption opportunities, aligning with what our members appreciate the most.”

Etihad Guest members can take advantage of the exclusive redemption fares and discounts on flights across Etihad’s expanding network, including the 12 exciting new destinations the airline has recently announced across Asia and Africa, such as Dhaka, Shanghai, Accra and Lagos.

To learn more about the Etihad Guest programme, visit etihad.com/etihadguest

About Etihad Airways

Etihad Airways, the national airline of the UAE, was formed in 2003 and quickly went on to become one of the world’s leading airlines. From its home in Abu Dhabi, Etihad flies to passenger and cargo destinations in the Middle East, Africa, Europe, Asia, Australia, and North America. Together with Etihad’s codeshare partners, Etihad’s network offers access to hundreds of international destinations. In recent years, Etihad has received numerous awards for its superior service and products, cargo offering, loyalty programme and more. To learn more, visit etihad.com.

For further details:

Duty Media Officer

Etihad Airways

data.title: Bitcoin Rises But Iran War Uncertainty Keeps Gains Limited — Market Talk
data.description:
0947 ET - Bitcoin rises slightly but remains stuck in a relatively narrow range as uncertainty over the Iran war caps gains. The cryptocurrency reached a one-week low of $74,962 on Wednesday, LSEG data, after the Federal Reserve's decision to leave rates unchanged and following a media report that the U.S. was considering fresh strikes against Iran. Bitcoin is last up 0.9% at $76,304. However, it continues to trade below $80,000, Trade Nation's David Morrison says in a note. It is too early to tell if $80,000 marks a peak for bitcoin following its recent strong rally, or whether traders are waiting for an opportunity to push it back above that level, he says. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0945 ET - Canada's economy continues its streak of decent economic data, with GDP climbing 0.2% in February from the prior month, says Benjamin Reitzes, economist at BMO Capital Markets. Reitzes, however, offers a caveat, noting only eight of 20 sectors tracked posted growth in February, "highlighting that the economy continues to struggle despite the decent headline." Meanwhile, Canada's nonfarm payrolls report suggests that employers shed over 60,000 jobs in February--which underscores BOC's description of labor market as soft, Reitzes adds. All told, "the economy continues to hang in there despite the ongoing dark cloud of trade uncertainty," Reitzes says, adding the data does not move the needle for the Bank of Canada when it comes to rates. (Paul.Vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

0936 ET - TD Securities describes Canadian GDP data for February as somewhat soft. While GDP rose 0.2%, it notes that Statistics Canada projects a flat reading for March. The firm says that puts 1Q annualized GDP close to the Bank of Canada's projection for a 1.5% rise. The BOC this week said it believes its policy rate will likely stay close to its current 2.25% level so long as the economy evolves as forecast. The GDP data, signaling a flat March, "puts a damper on what has otherwise been a strong 2-3 months for CAD activity data," the firm says. TD believes the BOC will remain on hold through 2026. (Paul.Vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

0935 ET - A somewhat encouraging GDP report for February adds to the clearance the Bank of Canada has to start considering interest-rate increases amid elevated energy prices, says economist Bradley Saunders of Capital Economics. GDP rose 0.2% in February from the prior month, and Statistics Canada now estimates 1.7% annualized growth in 1Q. "With the economic backdrop notably improved for a net oil exporter like Canada, and the Bank of Canada sounding increasingly uneasy about a sustained rise in oil prices, the odds of a return to rate hikes before year-end have risen notably," Saunders says. (Paul.Vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

0935 ET - The Japanese yen's decent recovery from earlier sharp falls could possibly reflect interventions from the Ministry of Finance to support the currency, TD Securities strategists say in a note. The dollar falls more than 2% to a two-month low of 155.54 yen after earlier reaching a 21-month high of 160.72, LSEG data show. The earlier break above the key 160 level likely led to currency interventions, they say. "We had cautioned increased intervention risk during local holiday period in Japan." Past interventions have driven the dollar-yen lower by around five big figures before grinding higher again, they say. Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said Thursday that the time is approaching to take "decisive action" in the currency market. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0934 ET - Canada's economy remained on shaky ground through the first quarter, though GDP and payroll data are consistent with an economy unlikely to produce excess inflationary pressures, Desjardins' Royce Mendes says. GDP advanced 0.2% in February but growth faded in March. Mendes sees GDP by expenditure and by industry tracking roughly 1.5% growth for 1Q, in line with the Bank of Canada's projection. Given a weak handoff, the economist agrees with the central bank that another quarter of roughly 1.5% growth is coming in 2Q.(robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

0932 ET - A rebound in the auto sector helped lift Canada GDP in February, and early estimates indicate output rose 1.7% annualized in 1Q--or a tad above the Bank of Canada's estimate. CIBC Capital Markets economist Andrew Grantham points out that, overall, the GDP report was a mixed bag, with manufacturing-sector strength offsetting weakness elsewhere. Nine of 16 components tracked recorded declines, led by construction and the public sector. Even with this week's jump in crude-oil prices, Grantham says there's enough slack in the economy to keep core inflation fairly muted, and that underpins CIBC's call for the Bank of Canada to hold the policy rate steady through 2026. (Paul.Vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

0925 ET - Bank of England policymakers voted 8-1 to keep its key rate at 3.75%. Governor Andrew Bailey, who voted to hold, noted that the energy-price shock will prompt a trade off between higher inflation and softer economic output. He places most weight on the bank's middle scenario that emphasizes some second-round effects, but also some weight on a more severe scenario that requires stronger monetary-policy response, according to the meeting's minutes. By contrast, Chief Economist Huw Pill, who voted for a 4% bank rate, says the uncertainty from the Middle East conflict is unlikely to dissipate soon. "Second-round effects in price and wage-setting stemming from this shock have the potential to raise U.K. inflation beyond the near term in a persistent manner," he says. (edward.frankl@wsj.com)

0923 ET - Bond markets will watch the Bank of England's next move closely as changing expectations for future policy rates have driven sharp swings in gilt yields, BlackRock Investment Institute's Vivek Paul says in a note. The BOE left policy rates on hold, as expected, on Thursday, but flagged a risk of higher rates ahead. Money markets price in a potential for three rate hikes this year, LSEG data show. Over the last two months, the gap between short- and long-term yields--a gauge of the compensation investors demand for holding longer-term U.K. gilts--has been steady, the U.K. chief investment strategist says. BlackRock stays neutral U.K. gilts on a six- to 12-month horizon. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0918 ET - Expectations for Bank of England interest rate rises look overly aggressive, Ebury strategist Matthew Ryan says in a note after the BOE held rates at 3.75% Thursday. "Unlike the 2022 inflationary episode, the current oil supply shock is set to prove temporary, and slack in the labor market should act to contain any second-order effects." The BOE is keeping its options open but this isn't a commitment to raise rates, he says. There could be some modest tightening if data warrant it but rates should remain unchanged for now given the fragility of the economy and jobs markets, he says. The market prices 62 basis points of rate rises by year-end compared to 78bps before the BOE's decision, LSEG data show.(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0917 ET - In light of incomplete data and an unclear geopolitical situation, it was a sensible for the European Central to leave the deposit rate unchanged at 2.0%, says DWS's Ulrike Kastens in a note. New projections are due in June, which should provide greater clarity on the impact of high energy prices on growth and inflation, the senior economist says. "We see the balance of risks shifting towards an interest rate hike," she says. Inflation is running at 3.0% and is expected to rise further, she says. DWS expects the ECB to act prudently rather than hastily, Kastens says. Money markets price in 69 basis points of ECB rate hikes this year, versus 75 basis points before the ECB's decision, according to LSEG. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0913 ET - Robust U.S. indicators push Treasury yields higher, although they remain below overnight levels. Fist-quarter annualized GDP growth hits 2%, up from the 4Q's 0.5% pace and near WSJ consensus of 2.2%. March PCE inflation accelerates as expected, to 3.5% from 2.8%. Weekly jobless claims decline more than forecast, to 189,000 from a revised 215,000, indicating layoffs remain low. Markets react overnight to talks of potential government intervention to support the Japanese yen. The WSJ Dollar Index falls 0.6%, with the greenback weakening more than 2% against the yen. The 10-year yield sits at 4.402%, down from around 4.432% overnight but off early lows. The two-year is at 3.906%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

data.title: Acadian Asset Management Inc reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Acadian Asset Management Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 68 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 54 cents. The mean expectation of three analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 89 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 83 cents to 94 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 39.3% to $167.00 million from a year ago; analysts expected $152.10 million.

  • Acadian Asset Management Inc shares had risen by 19.6% this quarter and gained 38.5% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 3.1% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, one analyst negatively revised an earnings estimate

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is no "strong buy" or "buy," 3 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the investment management & fund operators peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Acadian Asset Management Inc is $59.00, about 10.3% below its last closing price of $65.08

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data April 30 at 01:47 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: International Paper Co reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• International Paper Co reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 15 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 23 cents. The mean expectation of eleven analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 14 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 6 cents to 21 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 1.2% to $5.97 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $6.01 billion.

  • International Paper Co's reported EPS for the quarter was 11 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $60 million.

  • International Paper Co shares had fallen by 5.9% this quarter and lost 14.7% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 44.2% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, six analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 6 "strong buy" or "buy," 6 "hold" and 1 "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the paper packaging peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for International Paper Co is $42.00, about 20% above its last closing price of $33.58

data.description:

data.title: APD: EPS up 19% with margin expansion; guidance raised amid strong volumes and resilient supply
data.description:
Second quarter fiscal 2026 saw 19% EPS growth and margin expansion, driven by strong onsite and electronics volumes, productivity, and resilient helium supply despite Middle East disruptions. Full-year EPS guidance was raised to 8–10% growth, with robust project backlog and disciplined capital allocation.

data.title: AFL: Strong Q1 with robust Japan sales, solid U.S. growth, and continued capital returns
data.description:
Q1 2026 saw strong sales growth in Japan (+25.5%) and steady U.S. performance, with adjusted EPS up 6.6% year-over-year. Capital ratios remain robust, $1.3 billion was returned to shareholders, and new reinsurance initiatives in Japan are expected to drive future growth.

data.title: Trane Technologies PLC reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Trane Technologies PLC reported quarterly adjusted earnings of $2.63​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $2.45. The mean expectation of twenty one analysts for the quarter was for earnings of $2.53 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from $2.49 to $2.71 per share.

  • Revenue rose 6% to $4.97 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $4.81 billion.

  • Trane Technologies PLC's reported EPS for the quarter was $2.62​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $584.4 million.

  • Trane Technologies PLC shares had risen by 15.0% this quarter and gained 23.2% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 0.7% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no negative revisions of earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 12 "strong buy" or "buy," 13 "hold" and 1 "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the electrical components & equipment peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Trane Technologies PLC is $490.00, about 2.2% above its last closing price of $479.37

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data April 30 at 01:42 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated.
